text: The surgeon aspirates fluid from a right-sided ovarian cyst using an aspiration needle, collecting 5 ml for cytopathology. The cyst opening is enlarged to introduce suction, allowing complete removal of the cystic fluid. The inner cystic layer is held with one instrument while the outer layer is stripped away using another instrument, demonstrating efficient dissection.
The surgeon carefully separates the cystic valve from the surrounding tissue up to its base, ensuring no part is left behind. Once the separation is complete, the telescope is prepared for change.

The surgeon positions the telescope in the left iliac fossa port and prepares to remove the ovarian cyst through the 10 mm umbilical port. A cloth is introduced via the umbilical port to facilitate the extraction of the cyst, which is visibly being removed.
